items.definition_en: A point guard is a basketball player who directs the team's offense.
items.example_en: The point guard quickly passed the ball to the forward.

items.definition_en: A shooting guard is a player who specializes in scoring points.
items.example_en: The shooting guard hit several three-pointers during the game.

items.definition_en: A center is usually the tallest player who plays near the basket.
items.example_en: The center grabbed a crucial rebound in the final minute.

items.definition_en: A power forward plays close to the basket and is good at rebounding.
items.example_en: The power forward dominated the boards all night long.

items.definition_en: A small forward is a versatile player who can score and defend.
items.example_en: The small forward scored twenty points in the first half.

items.definition_en: A possession is when a team controls the ball.
items.example_en: Their possession ended with a successful jump shot.

items.definition_en: A turnover is when a team loses the ball to the other team.
items.example_en: A late turnover cost them the game.

items.definition_en: A fast break is a quick attack after gaining possession.
items.example_en: They scored easily on a fast break.

items.definition_en: A three pointer is a shot made from beyond the three-point line.
items.example_en: He celebrated after making a long three pointer.

items.definition_en: An assist is a pass that leads directly to a score.
items.example_en: She led the team in assists last season.

items.definition_en: A rebound is when a player retrieves the ball after a missed shot.
items.example_en: He grabbed the rebound and started a new play.

items.definition_en: A dribble is the act of bouncing the ball while moving.
items.example_en: His dribble allowed him to get past the defender.

items.definition_en: A block is a defensive move that stops a shot.
items.example_en: The block by the center prevented an easy basket.

items.definition_en: A layup is a close-range shot made by driving to the basket.
items.example_en: She made a layup right as the buzzer sounded.

items.definition_en: A jump shot is taken while jumping into the air.
items.example_en: He nailed a jump shot from the free throw line.

items.definition_en: A court is the playing area for a basketball game.
items.example_en: They ran onto the court for the big match.

items.definition_en: A free throw is an unopposed shot awarded after a foul.
items.example_en: He missed his second free throw under pressure.

items.definition_en: A backboard is the flat board behind the basket.
items.example_en: The ball bounced off the backboard and into the net.

items.definition_en: A referee is the official who enforces the rules during a game.
items.example_en: The referee called a foul on the defender.

items.definition_en: A shot clock is a timer measuring team possession before a shot.
items.example_en: They rushed their play because the shot clock was expiring.

items.definition_en: A bank shot is a shot that is bounced off the backboard and into the net.
items.example_en: Instead of a layup, he scored with a beautiful bank shot.

items.definition_en: A hoop is the ring that players try to throw the ball through.
items.example_en: He made a basket by shooting the ball through the hoop.
